chapter_title.ar: بَابُ مَنْ أَكَلَ أَوْ شَرِبَ وَ هُوَ شَاكٌّ فِي الْفَجْرِ أَوْ بَعْدَ طُلُوعِهِ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 17 – The one who eats or drinks and he doubts regarding the dawn, or after its emergence

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (6/262)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (6/262)
text: هُ سُئِلَ عَنْ رَجُلٍ تَسَحَّرَ ثُمَّ خَرَجَ مِنْ بَيْتِهِ وَ قَدْ طَلَعَ الْفَجْرُ وَ تَبَيَّنَ قَالَ يُتِمُّ صَوْمَهُ ذَلِكَ ثُمَّ لْيَقْضِهِ فَإِنْ تَسَحَّرَ فِي غَيْرِ شَهْرِ رَمَضَانَ بَعْدَ الْفَجْرِ أَفْطَرَ

ثُمَّ قَالَ إِنَّ أَبِي كَانَ لَيْلَةً يُصَلِّي وَ أَنَا آكُلُ فَانْصَرَفَ فَقَالَ أَمَّا جَعْفَرٌ فَقَدْ أَكَلَ وَ شَرِبَ بَعْدَ الْفَجْرِ فَأَمَرَنِي فَأَفْطَرْتُ ذَلِكَ الْيَوْمَ فِي غَيْرِ شَهْرِ رَمَضَانَ .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, from Ibn Abu Umeyr, from Hammad, from Al Halby,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> having been asked about a man who partook a pre-dawn meal, then went out from his house, and the dawn had emerged and it was clear. He<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘He should complete that Fast of his, then let him make up for it. So, if he had taken the pre-dawn meal during other than a Month of Ramazan after the dawn, so he should break’.

Then he<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘My<sup>asws</sup> father<sup>asws</sup> was Praying one night, and I<sup>asws</sup> ate. So he<sup>asws</sup> completed and said: ‘As for Ja’far<sup>asws</sup>, so he<sup>asws</sup> has eaten and drunk after the dawn’. So he<sup>asws</sup> instructed me<sup>asws</sup>, so I<sup>asws</sup> broke the Fast of that day (which was) other than the Month of Ramazan’.
en.sarwar: 1. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from his father from ibn abu ‘Umayr from Hammad from al-Halabiy who has said the following: “I once asked abu ‘ Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, about a man who eats Suhur (breakfast in the morning before dawn), then comes out of his house to find out when it has already become dawn. He (the Imam) said, ‘He is required to complete that fast then make Qada’ (compensatory fast) for it; however if such breakfast is in a time other than the month of Ramadan and after dawn one must not fast.’ He (the Imam) then said, ‘Once during the night my father was performing Salat (prayer) and I was eating Suhur (breakfast in the morning before dawn). He ended and said, ‘Ja’far has eaten and drunk after dawn.’ So he commanded me and I did not fast that day which was not of the month of Ramadan.”’
